old_claim.note: Basic Chemistry (Early Forms) is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained between Compass and basic_chemistry_early_forms; The magnetic compass source supports lodestone and magnetized needles, not a dependency on the broad Classical basic-chemistry node.

source_shape.source_locator: History section: mariners in China and Europe discovered lodestone/needle direction-finding for navigation in the 12th century.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The magnetic compass source supports lodestone and magnetized needles, not a dependency on the broad Classical basic-chemistry node.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports the scoped Compass claim or adjacent chronology without requiring basic_chemistry_early_forms as a prerequisite.

ontology_before: Compass treated basic_chemistry_early_forms as a direct prerequisite.
ontology_after: Compass no longer directly depends on basic_chemistry_early_forms; the dependency boundary is narrowed to operationally necessary predecessors.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: compass must not directly depend on basic_chemistry_early_forms unless a narrower source-backed node scope is introduced.
would_reject_if: A source shows compass is intentionally scoped only to cases that require basic_chemistry_early_forms.
The graph splits compass and reserves this id for a narrower technology that directly depends on basic_chemistry_early_forms.
A stronger source demonstrates that basic_chemistry_early_forms is chronologically and causally necessary for compass.
short_reason: The magnetic compass source supports lodestone and magnetized needles, not a dependency on the broad Classical basic-chemistry node.
